next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture with measurement of opening pressure and cerebrospinal fluid analysis (cell count, protein, glucose, culture, cryptococcal antigen, cytology)
Obtain MRI of the brain with magnetic resonance venography (MRV)
Evaluate for coagulopathy and potential hypercoagulable state due to cirrhosis
Consult neurology and ophthalmology for assessment of papilledema and cranial nerve involvement
Review all current medications for potential drug-induced headache or secondary intracranial hypertension

differential_diagnoses: Increased intracranial pressure secondary to hepatic encephalopathy (cerebral edema)
next_steps: Perform immediate fundoscopy to assess for papilledema
Obtain MRI brain with MRV to evaluate for venous thrombosis and structural abnormalities
Evaluate coagulation profile (INR, platelets, fibrinogen) to assess for safe performance of lumbar puncture
Perform lumbar puncture with measurement of opening pressure and CSF analysis (cryptococcal antigen, cytology, protein, glucose, cell count, culture)
Consult neurology and ophthalmology for emergent assessment
Review metabolic panel (especially electrolytes and magnesium) and medications for potential iatrogenic triggers
